what_it_does: This file is a workflow script that orchestrates a single-run, multi-agent build for the V-A registry-generation chain. It runs a Build phase where six file-disjoint pieces (RG1 extract, RG3 register_element role, RG6 confirm, RG7 cascade spec, RG8 proposal surface, RG9 writeback) are produced in parallel, followed by a Commit phase that gates the results, updates the roles drift-home, commits each piece with explicit pathspecs, and records status.
observations: The workflow is declared as a unified replacement for two separate session loops.
It defines shared BINDING LAWS that every build agent must follow.
It defines a BUILD_SCHEMA that each agent must return.
Build pieces are labeled by lane: guided-review [M] for RG1/RG7/RG8/RG9 and cognition [C] for RG3/RG6.
RG3 is explicitly framed as a cognition-sharpened draft, asking for richer nested Pydantic schemas, context efficiency, and unification with existing roles.
RG6 is designed as a two-layer confirmation: a deterministic refcheck floor plus a jury accuracy layer with a declared verdict rule.
The workflow forbids agents from touching real design/_system/addresses.json or real mockups; writeback must be verified on temporary copies only.
The Commit phase constructs a manifest from build reports, updates roles/AGENTS.md for new roles, runs the company gate, and commits per piece using explicit pathspecs.
standouts: A single workflow converges both the guided-review and cognition lanes in one run.
The candidates.json schema is called out as the explicit seam contract between RG1 extraction and RG3 registration.
The confirm step uses a deterministic no-fiction floor (refcheck) underneath a model jury to avoid epistemic monoculture.
Writeback is strictly operator-gated and temp-copy verified.
Commits are per-piece with explicit pathspecs to avoid concurrent-index races.
conventions: Follows the workflow DSL pattern: export const meta, phase(), parallel(), agent(), log().
Uses JSON Schema to validate agent outputs.
Follows file-disjoint ownership and lane labeling.
Follows a reuse-not-parallel law: extend parse.py, mirror screen_reader.py, reuse refcheck.py.
Breaks the convention of small prompts by embedding very large, law-heavy prompt strings inline.
Breaks build/release separation by including commit orchestration inside the same workflow file.
concerns: Inline prompts are long and duplicate the LAWS block, making updates error-prone.
The workflow references external commands and tools without defining their interfaces in this file.
The anti-concurrency rule is procedural, not mechanically enforced.
The header comment says it is safe to run as-is if not refined, which may discourage review.
RG8 cannot be render-verified in parallel due to Chrome leak risk, so runtime UI behavior is deferred.
notes: A dense, law-heavy orchestration script that tries to build an auditable registry-generation pipeline in one coordinated pass with explicit human approval gates.
questions: What runtime environment provides agent, phase, parallel, and log?
What are the exact file paths and schemas for roles/screen_reader.py, design/_system/refcheck.py, and the Feature & Function Inventory?
How does the resource manager resolve company gpu and company up chat-4b --wait co-residency?
What happens when the gate reports pre-existing reds unrelated to these pieces?
What is the exact shape of a run:// address used in input_addresses wiring?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to provide a unified single-run workflow for the whole registry-generation chain, and the file content matches that purpose.
apparent_themes: Convergence in a single run instead of negotiated loops.
Operator approval before mutating real design artifacts.
File-disjoint parallel work with explicit seam contracts.
intention_signals: The file is trying to create a trustworthy, auditable, human-gated pipeline that extracts UI elements, proposes registry dossiers, confirms them against real capabilities, and writes them back only after explicit approval.
novelty: It is a workflow file that doubles as a detailed multi-agent prompt harness, embedding build instructions, epistemic safeguards, and commit orchestration in one place.
contribution: It contributes a unified phase structure, an explicit seam contract between extraction and registration, a two-layer confirmation pattern, an operator-only writeback floor, and a per-piece explicit-pathspec commit discipline.
summary_for_embedding: A unified multi-agent workflow script that builds the V-A registry-generation chain in parallel file-disjoint pieces and then gates, reflects, and commits each piece separately.
intention_for_embedding: To replace separate guided-review and cognition loops with one coordinated run that produces extractors, roles, confirmation, cascade spec, UI surface, and writeback modules under strict no-fiction and operator-approval rules.
